funfield 0.1.0

    • Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the coworker assertiveness ESJT

    • Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the speeding EXSJT

Emit the funfield decimal-house-style CSS once per HTML document

Source: R/f0.R
strip0_css.Rd

Outputs a small <style> block that turns on font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums for any right-aligned table cell. Combined with right-alignment (which kable0 enables for originally-numeric columns and group_kable enables via its own CSS), this makes the decimal points line up under each other even when integer parts differ in width (e.g., .664 versus -4.654).

Intended to be called once per vignette, in a chunk with echo = FALSE, results = "asis". Re-emitting it is harmless but wasteful.

Usage

strip0_css()

Value

Side effect: prints raw HTML. Invisibly returns the CSS string.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# In a vignette setup chunk: ```{r echo=FALSE, results="asis"}
#                            strip0_css()
#                            ```
strip0_css()
} # }
